why_good: Paired text and drawing answering a cause-effect prompt ('How would your life be different if dinosaurs were not extinct?'). Single well-formed sentence using hypothetical 'would' and 'because' connector. Drawing directly illustrates the written response — house with fence as safety, dinosaur as threat outside. Demonstrates cross-modal production: text and visual working together.
transferable_pattern: Cross-modal cause-effect reasoning: producing complementary text and visual output that reinforce the same argument. The text states the consequence; the drawing visualizes the spatial logic (inside/safe vs outside/threat).
tradeoffs: Short (15 words) — a school worksheet prompt constrains length. The drawing carries more analytical weight than the text alone.
review_criteria: Observed artifact (evidence tier 4). School worksheet with original handwriting and drawing. Decoded text confirms spelling patterns consistent with profile.
